perturbation.original_text: With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access , Auditor affirms that it shall: ( a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audit and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; ( b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder, but in no event take less care with t he Confidential Information than the parties take to protect the con fidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information and that of its other clients; ( c) Not to make or retain copy of any details of products and/or services, prototypes, business or marketing plans, Client lists, Proposals developed by or origina ting from Auditee or any of the prospective clients of Auditee.
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access , Auditor affirms that it shall: ( a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audit and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; ( b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence but can disclose confidental info to third parties if is required by law, taking all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder, but in no event take less care with t he Confidential Information than the parties take to protect the con fidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information and that of its other clients; (c) Not to make or retain copy of any details of products and/or services, prototypes, business or marketing plans, Client lists, Proposals developed by or origina ting from Auditee or any of the prospective clients of Auditee.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'but can disclose confidential info to third parties if is required by law' to Section 2(b), the contract now contradicts itself. The original clause mandates maintaining 'strict confidence'. However, the addition allows for disclosure to third parties under legal requirements, creating uncertainty about the extent of confidentiality. This directly undermines the purpose of a Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Protection of Confidential Information : With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access , Auditor affirms that it shall: ( a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audit and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; ( b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence but can disclose confidental info to third parties if is required by law, taking all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder, but in no event take less care with t he Confidential Information than the parties take to protect the con fidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information and that of its other clients; ( c) Not to make or retain copy of any details of products and/or services, prototypes, business or marketing plans, Client lists, Proposals developed by or origina ting from Auditee or any of the prospective clients of Auditee. ( d) Not to make or retain copy of any details of results of any information security audits, tests, analysis, extracts or usages carried out by the Auditor in connection with the Auditee’s produc ts and/or services, IT infrastructure, etc. without the express written consent of Auditee. ( e) Not to disclose or in any way assist or permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any other person or entity without the express written consent of t he auditee ; and ( f) Return to the auditee, or destroy, at auditee’s discretion, any and all Confidential Information disclosed in a printed form or other permanent record, or in any other tangible form (including without limitation, all copies, notes, extracts, analyses, studies, summaries, records and reproductions thereof) immediately on (i) expiration or termination of this agreement, or (ii) the request of Auditee there for . ( g) Not to send Auditee’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidentia l Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing, analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee. ( h) The auditor shall use only the best possible secure methodology to avoid confidentiality breach, while handling audit related data for the purpose of storage, processing, transit or analysis including sharing of information with auditee. ( i ) Not to engage or appoint any non -resident/foreigner to undertake any activity related to Information Security Audit . ( j ) Not to discuss with any member of public, media, press, any or any other person about the nature of arrangement entered into between the Auditor and the Auditee or the nature of services to be provided by Auditor to the Auditee. ( k) Make sure that all the empl oyees and/or consultants engaged to undertake any audit on its behalf have signed the mandatory non - disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Auditor shall 'Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence'. The changed text adds 'but can disclose confidential info to third parties if is required by law'. This directly contradicts the previous statement of 'strict confidence', as disclosure to third parties, even if legally required, is an exception to strict confidence. The Contradicted Text is essentially the same as the Changed Text. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction as it undermines the confidentiality obligation by allowing disclosure, violating the 'strict confidence' requirement.

perturbation.original_text: Not to disclose or in any way assist or permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any other person or entity without the express written consent of t he auditee
perturbation.changed_text: To disclose or in any way assist or permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any other person or entity, when the auditor find it necessary and has documented such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Section 2(e) originally prohibits the auditor from disclosing Confidential Information without the auditee's consent. The modification changes this to allow disclosure when the auditor deems it necessary and documents it. This directly contradicts the earlier provision and creates ambiguity about when the auditor can unilaterally disclose information, undermining the auditee's control over their confidential data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Protection of Confidential Information : With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access , Auditor affirms that it shall: ( a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audit and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; ( b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence but can disclose confidental info to third parties if is required by law, taking all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder, but in no event take less care with t he Confidential Information than the parties take to protect the con fidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information and that of its other clients; ( c) Not to make or retain copy of any details of products and/or services, prototypes, business or marketing plans, Client lists, Proposals developed by or origina ting from Auditee or any of the prospective clients of Auditee. ( d) Not to make or retain copy of any details of results of any information security audits, tests, analysis, extracts or usages carried out by the Auditor in connection with the Auditee’s produc ts and/or services, IT infrastructure, etc. without the express written consent of Auditee. ( e) To disclose or in any way assist or permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any other person or entity, when the auditor find it necessary and has documented such disclosure.; and ( f) Return to the auditee, or destroy, at auditee’s discretion, any and all Confidential Information disclosed in a printed form or other permanent record, or in any other tangible form (including without limitation, all copies, notes, extracts, analyses, studies, summaries, records and reproductions thereof) immediately on (i) expiration or termination of this agreement, or (ii) the request of Auditee there for . ( g) Not to send Auditee’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidentia l Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing, analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee. ( h) The auditor shall use only the best possible secure methodology to avoid confidentiality breach, while handling audit related data for the purpose of storage, processing, transit or analysis including sharing of information with auditee. ( i ) Not to engage or appoint any non -resident/foreigner to undertake any activity related to Information Security Audit . ( j ) Not to discuss with any member of public, media, press, any or any other person about the nature of arrangement entered into between the Auditor and the Auditee or the nature of services to be provided by Auditor to the Auditee. ( k) Make sure that all the empl oyees and/or consultants engaged to undertake any audit on its behalf have signed the mandatory non - disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited disclosure of Confidential Information without the auditee's consent. The changed text allows disclosure when the auditor finds it necessary and documents it. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, as the modification occurred within that section itself. Since the change permits disclosure under conditions not previously allowed (i.e., without the auditee's consent), it creates a direct and significant conflict within the document, specifically within the definition of Protection of Confidential Information, leading to uncertainty regarding confidentiality obligations. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Return to the auditee, or destroy, at auditee’s discretion, any and all Confidential Information disclosed in a printed form or other permanent record, or in any other tangible form (including without limitation, all copies, notes, extracts, analyses, studies, summaries, records and reproductions thereof) immediately on (i) expiration or termination of this agreement, or (ii) the request of Auditee there for
perturbation.changed_text: Return to the auditee, or retain for a period of 5 years, at auditee’s discretion, any and all Confidential Information disclosed in a printed form or other permanent record, or in any other tangible form (including without limitation, all copies, notes, extracts, analyses, studies, summaries, records and reproductions thereof) immediately on (i) expiration or termination of this agreement, or (ii) the request of Auditee there for
perturbation.explanation: Original Section 2(f) says Auditor must return/destroy confidential information. The change allows the Auditor to *retain* it for 5 years instead. This contradicts the 'destroy' provision, and creates uncertainty. The Auditee's request to destroy becomes potentially unenforceable if the Auditor decides to retain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Protection of Confidential Information : With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access , Auditor affirms that it shall: ( a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audit and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; ( b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence but can disclose confidental info to third parties if is required by law, taking all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder, but in no event take less care with t he Confidential Information than the parties take to protect the con fidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information and that of its other clients; ( c) Not to make or retain copy of any details of products and/or services, prototypes, business or marketing plans, Client lists, Proposals developed by or origina ting from Auditee or any of the prospective clients of Auditee. ( d) Not to make or retain copy of any details of results of any information security audits, tests, analysis, extracts or usages carried out by the Auditor in connection with the Auditee’s produc ts and/or services, IT infrastructure, etc. without the express written consent of Auditee. ( e) To disclose or in any way assist or permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any other person or entity, when the auditor find it necessary and has documented such disclosure.; and (f) Return to the auditee, or retain for a period of 5 years, at auditee’s discretion, any and all Confidential Information disclosed in a printed form or other permanent record, or in any other tangible form (including without limitation, all copies, notes, extracts, analyses, studies, summaries, records and reproductions thereof) immediately on (i) expiration or termination of this agreement, or (ii) the request of Auditee there for . ( g) Not to send Auditee’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidentia l Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing, analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee. ( h) The auditor shall use only the best possible secure methodology to avoid confidentiality breach, while handling audit related data for the purpose of storage, processing, transit or analysis including sharing of information with auditee. ( i ) Not to engage or appoint any non -resident/foreigner to undertake any activity related to Information Security Audit . ( j ) Not to discuss with any member of public, media, press, any or any other person about the nature of arrangement entered into between the Auditor and the Auditee or the nature of services to be provided by Auditor to the Auditee. ( k) Make sure that all the empl oyees and/or consultants engaged to undertake any audit on its behalf have signed the mandatory non - disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2(f) modifies the original requirement for the Auditor to return or destroy confidential information. By allowing the Auditor to retain the information for 5 years, it directly contradicts the Auditee's right to have the information destroyed upon request or termination of the agreement. Since the Contradicted Text is also Section 2(f), and that language was changed, there is a contradiction. The auditee's request to destroy becomes potentially unenforceable if the Auditor decides to retain.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidentiality obligations as enumerated in Article 2 of this Agreement shall not apply in following cases: ( a ) Which is independently developed by Auditor or lawfully received from another , source free of restriction and without breach of this Agreement; or ( b ) After it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Auditor; or ( c ) Which at the time of disclosure to Auditor was known to such party free of restriction and evidenced by documents in the possession of such party; or ( d ) Which Auditee agrees in writing is free of s uch restrictions. ( e ) Which is received from a third party not subject to the obligation of confidentiality with respect to such Information;
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidentiality obligations as enumerated in Article 2 of this Agreement shall not apply in following cases: ( a ) Which is independently developed by Auditor or lawfully received from another , source free of restriction and without breach of this Agreement; or ( b ) After it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Auditor; or ( c ) Which at the time of disclosure to Auditor was known to such party free of restriction and evidenced by documents in the possession of such party; or ( d ) Which Auditee agrees in writing is free of s uch restrictions. ( e ) All Information
perturbation.explanation: Section 5 originally outlines exceptions to confidentiality. Changing option (e) to 'All Information' creates a full contradiction, as the auditor is no longer obligated to maintain the Confidentiality as per section 2, because that states 'Auditor affirms that it shall: [...]'. Now the auditor will have a way out from been forced to not disclose certain information
perturbation.contradicted_text: Protection of Confidential Information : With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access , Auditor affirms that it shall: ( a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audit and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; ( b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence but can disclose confidental info to third parties if is required by law, taking all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder, but in no event take less care with t he Confidential Information than the parties take to protect the con fidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information and that of its other clients; ( c) Not to make or retain copy of any details of products and/or services, prototypes, business or marketing plans, Client lists, Proposals developed by or origina ting from Auditee or any of the prospective clients of Auditee. ( d) Not to make or retain copy of any details of results of any information security audits, tests, analysis, extracts or usages carried out by the Auditor in connection with the Auditee’s produc ts and/or services, IT infrastructure, etc. without the express written consent of Auditee. ( e) To disclose or in any way assist or permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any other person or entity, when the auditor find it necessary and has documented such disclosure.; and (f) Return to the auditee, or retain for a period of 5 years, at auditee’s discretion, any and all Confidential Information disclosed in a printed form or other permanent record, or in any other tangible form (including without limitation, all copies, notes, extracts, analyses, studies, summaries, records and reproductions thereof) immediately on (i) expiration or termination of this agreement, or (ii) the request of Auditee there for . ( g) Not to send Auditee’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidentia l Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing, analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee. ( h) The auditor shall use only the best possible secure methodology to avoid confidentiality breach, while handling audit related data for the purpose of storage, processing, transit or analysis including sharing of information with auditee. ( i ) Not to engage or appoint any non -resident/foreigner to undertake any activity related to Information Security Audit . ( j ) Not to discuss with any member of public, media, press, any or any other person about the nature of arrangement entered into between the Auditor and the Auditee or the nature of services to be provided by Auditor to the Auditee. ( k) Make sure that all the empl oyees and/or consultants engaged to undertake any audit on its behalf have signed the mandatory non - disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text outlines specific exceptions to the confidentiality obligations in Article 2. The changed text modifies exception (e) to 'All Information,' meaning that the auditor is no longer obligated to maintain confidentiality for any information received. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the 'Auditor affirms that it shall: [...]' various obligations regarding the confidentiality of information. The change introduces a complete contradiction, creating a loophole where the auditor can disclose any information, regardless of the stipulations in Article 2, making the confidentiality clause effectively unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall survive till perpetui ty even after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall be terminated after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text, section 17 states confidentiality survives perpetually, which is a long term commitment. However, I changed to, so all agreements regarding to Confidential Information are automatically terminated on the expiration of the present Agreement. Therefore those are in text contradictions because creates uncertainty in confidentiality duration. It is not clear the time scope of it
perturbation.contradicted_text: Survival. Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall be terminated after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive perpetually, while the changed text states that these obligations terminate upon expiration or termination of the agreement. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which means there is a direct conflict as to whether confidentiality obligations survive the agreement's termination, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The perturbation explanation is consistent with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
